Do you want me to enhance the LFSCK to use the dt_sync() or you will do that by yourself in another patch?

Patch Set 3:

Fan Yong, I can add in my patch, but I'm not sure how to test that properly. any suggestion?

Patch Set 3:

Alex: In fact, we have met the sync issues in current sanity-lfsck tests, please check test_18d comment "LU-3469: ...". So dropping the "sync; sleep 2" after such comment can verify whether your patch works or not. That is the simplest way.

(2 comments)

I think it would be better to make a single /proc "sync_changes" entry. Write forces sync, read returns the number of unsyc'd changes as it does now.

Patch Set 6:

I'm a bit surprised that patch passes own test.. in my local testing (at least) the original test didn't pass when dt_sync() isn't called.

Patch Set 6:

Alex: no surprise, the patch passed its own test because the lproc interface ("sync_changes") has been registered already by the existing code. This patch only adds a write method to such lproc interface.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Sorry Alex I missed that. I was in the mind set it was new code.

commit.subject: LU-3469 osp: osp_sync() to flush pending changes synchronously
commit.message:
LU-3469 osp: osp_sync() to flush pending changes synchronously

also a test added to sanity to verify this.
